model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Nebraska is one of the strongest 4-seeds in the bracket, ranking 14th in KenPom with an adjusted efficiency margin of +26.12, a staggering 24 points higher than Troy's +1.74, driven by one of the nation's best defenses (AdjD 92.5, top 7 nationally) that should completely stifle Troy's below-average offense (AdjO 110.8, 141st nationally). Troy's Sun Belt-caliber roster, with a defense ranked 181st nationally, lacks the firepower or defensive identity to stay competitive against a 26-6 Big Ten team that has been battle-tested against elite competition all season. While the 4/13 seed line historically produces upsets roughly 20% of the time, the quality gap here is significantly wider than the typical 4/13 matchup, making Nebraska a very comfortable favorite.

reasoningStep.title: Nebraska vs Troy – South 4/13 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Nebraska's elite defense and massive efficiency margin advantage make this one of the more lopsided 4/13 matchups in the bracket.
evidence: Nebraska ranks 14th in KenPom (AdjEM +26.12) vs Troy at 143rd (AdjEM +1.74), a 24.4-point efficiency gap per 100 possessions
Nebraska's defense is elite at 92.5 AdjD (top 7 nationally), while Troy's offense (110.8, 141st) and defense (109.0, 181st) are both mediocre
Nebraska's 26-6 Big Ten record reflects far superior competition compared to Troy's 22-11 Sun Belt slate
Torvik rankings confirm the gap: Nebraska 19th vs Troy 142nd, with Nebraska's AdjDE ranked 7th nationally

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

Search is unavailable, but I have strong ratings data to work with. Let me analyze the matchup with what I have.

**Key Findings:**

- **Nebraska (4-seed):** KenPom #14, Torvik #19. Record: 26-6. AdjO: 118.6 (55th), AdjD: 92.5 (elite, top 7). Adj EM: +26.12. Big Ten conference.
- **Troy (13-seed):** KenPom #143, Torvik #142. Record: 22-11. AdjO: 110.8 (141st), AdjD: 109.0 (181st). Adj EM: +1.74. Sun Belt conference.

The efficiency margin gap is massive: +26.12 vs +1.74, a difference of ~24.4 points per 100 possessions. Nebraska's defense is elite (7th nationally in Torvik, AdjD 92.5 in KenPom) and should suffocate Troy's mediocre offense. Troy's defense (181st) will struggle to contain Nebraska's solid attack.
